hardware: Intel Core Raptor Lake i7, 12P+4E cores, DDR4-3200, 15.75 GB, Windows 10

quality.phase3_ppl_11_90_note: Not reproducible on current stack (138-189 WikiText-64 with same model/tokenizer). Historical run used all-LUT dispatch assignments; see phase10_baseline.json.

vs_llama_cpp.note: 128-token greedy decode, 8 threads, chat prompt <|user|>The<|end|><|assistant|>, --ignore-eos on both stacks. llama.cpp ~4x faster steady decode; ASDSL pays ~8.5 min quantize-at-load vs mmap GGUF.

techniques_implemented.lut_kernel: correct but not used at Phi-4 matrix sizes (L2 budget exceeded)
techniques_implemented.parallel_quantization: vectorized 9-candidate grid search (2x speedup at quant time)
techniques_implemented.sparse_gemv: implemented and correct; disabled by default (insufficient activation sparsity on Phi-4 for throughput gain)
techniques_implemented.learned_correction: implemented (32x MLP, 320KB); trained on synthetic data pending real calibration run
techniques_implemented.numpy_forward_stack: 32 layers in single numpy path, no per-layer torch (Phase 7)

key_learnings: LUT kernel O(1) depth advantage requires matrix to fit in L2 cache (~512KB). Phi-4 projections are 6-512MB per tile — LUT not applicable at this model size.
Dispatch overhead matters: Python routing added 0.5 tok/s regression until precomputed dispatch table was added (Phase 6).
The largest single gains came from fixing bugs (RoPE tables Phase 8/9, torch-numpy boundary Phase 7) not from new algorithms.
At 16-17% DDR4 bandwidth utilization, remaining headroom requires either fewer bytes (2-bit quantization) or wider SIMD (AVX-512 on compatible hardware).
WikiText-2 absolute PPL is a poor gate for instruct-tuned multimodal models; relative 4-bit vs FP16 on the same corpus is the correct quality metric.
On WikiText-64, 4-bit Q4 is ~44% worse PPL than FP16; 8-bit matches FP16. Absolute PPL<=12.40 (Phase 3) is not a valid target for this configuration.
